summaryText: Pastor Ryan Windauer opened by sharing his family's gratitude for Impact Church and the growth in the youth ministry, framing it as a 'good report' on the next generation. He then preached a message from Joshua chapter 1, declaring that 'It's Time to Move On.' Using the Israelites' transition from wandering to entering the Promised Land, Pastor Ryan explained how God's people can delay His promises through unbelief, disobedience, or getting stuck in the past. He emphasized that moving on requires letting go of old mindsets, patterns, and seasons that God has closed, just as Joshua had to accept that Moses was gone. The message offered three practical requirements for stepping into a new season: cultivating a new spirit of strength and courage, trusting in God's new supply (moving from 'manna' to 'milk and honey'), and taking new steps of faith and personal responsibility. Pastor Ryan closed with an invitation for those feeling stuck to surrender to God's new direction and for those who have never said 'yes' to Jesus to begin a relationship with Him.
mainPoints: To move into the new season God has for you, you must first let go of the old mindsets, grief, and patterns that are holding you back.
Stepping into God's promise requires a new spirit of strength and courage, recognizing that God gives us challenges bigger than ourselves so we learn to depend on Him.
God provides new supply for new seasons and calls us to take new, practical steps of faith, moving beyond prayer into obedient action.

versesMentioned.text: After the death of Moses the servant of the Lord, the Lord said to Joshua son of Nun, Moses’ aide: “Moses my servant is dead. Now then, you and all these people, get ready to cross the Jordan River into the land I am about to give to them—to the Israelites. I will give you every place where you set your foot, as I promised Moses. Your territory will extend from the desert to Lebanon, and from the great river, the Euphrates—all the Hittite country—to the Mediterranean Sea in the west. No one will be able to stand against you all the days of your life. As I was with Moses, so I will be with you; I will never leave you nor forsake you. Be strong and courageous, because you will lead these people to inherit the land I swore to their ancestors to give them.”
“Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.”
“But because my servant Caleb has a different spirit and follows me wholeheartedly, I will bring him into the land he went to, and his descendants will inherit it.”

The day after the Passover, that very day, they ate some of the produce of the land: unleavened bread and roasted grain. The manna stopped the day after they ate this food from the land; there was no longer any manna for the Israelites, but that year they ate the produce of Canaan.
“I will give you every place where you set your foot, as I promised Moses.”

Monday.scripture: Joshua 1:9 - “Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.”
Monday.action: Identify one old mindset or fear you are holding onto. Write it down and pray for God to replace it with His strength and courage.
Monday.prayer: God, I choose to be strong and courageous today. Replace my fear with faith, knowing You are with me. Give me a new spirit for this new season.

Tuesday.scripture: Joshua 5:11-12 - “The manna stopped... that year they ate the produce of Canaan.”
Tuesday.action: Reflect on one area where you've been settling for 'manna' (the old provision). Ask God to reveal the 'milk and honey' He has for you now.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your faithful provision. I trust that You have new and better things for me in this season. Change my appetite for the things of Your kingdom.

Wednesday.scripture: Joshua 1:3 - “I will give you every place where you set your foot...”
Wednesday.action: Identify one practical, faith-filled step God is asking you to take this week (e.g., a difficult conversation, signing up for baptism, starting a new habit). Commit to doing it.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I don't want to just pray for change; I want to step into it. Give me the faith to take the new step You have shown me today.

Thursday.scripture: Numbers 14:24 - “...my servant Caleb has a different spirit and follows me wholeheartedly...”
Thursday.action: When you face a challenge today, consciously choose to see it as Caleb did—not as an obstacle, but as an opportunity for God to show up.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me a 'different spirit' like Caleb's. Help me to see every challenge through the lens of Your promise and power.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 - “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.”
Friday.action: In an area where you feel weak or insufficient, tell God, 'I can't handle this, but You can.' Release the burden to Him.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I admit my weakness. I don't have what it takes, but Your grace is sufficient. I depend entirely on You today.

Saturday.scripture: Proverbs 23:7 - “For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.”
Saturday.action: Review the old mindset you identified on Monday. Declare a biblical truth over that area (e.g., 'I am more than a conqueror in Christ').
Saturday.prayer: God, transform my thinking. Where I have believed lies, plant Your truth. Prepare my heart and mind to fully step into the new thing You have for me.
